watermark: ⚠️ SYNTHETIC — AI-generated fictional session. Not a real person, not clinical advice.

turns.speaker: Therapist
turns.text: Hey Maya, good to see you. How's the connection — can you hear me okay?

turns.speaker: Client
turns.text: Yeah, fine. Sorry, just — give me a sec, closing Slack. Okay. I'm here.

turns.speaker: Therapist
turns.text: No rush. Last week you were going to try handing the launch checklist to Sam. How'd that land?

turns.speaker: Client
turns.text: I mean… I drafted the handoff doc. Then I sort of rewrote his part. It's probably fine, but I figured it'd be faster if I just— yeah.

turns.speaker: Therapist
turns.text: So the thought was 'it'll be faster if I do it myself.' What's the evidence for that?

turns.speaker: Client
turns.text: Honestly? It wasn't faster. I was up till one. I just— I couldn't stand the idea of it going out with a typo and it being on me.

turns.speaker: Therapist
turns.text: That sounds like the 'if it's not perfect it's on me' belief again. Want to put it through a thought record this week?

action_items: complete one thought record on the 'it's on me' belief
